data.title: Microsoft has some 'flexibility' in capex spending, KeyBanc says
data.description:
Amid reports that Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) is slowing or pausing some of its artificial intelligence-related capex spending, KeyBanc Capital Markets said the tech giant has some “flexibility” in how it spends its money.

“Construction and data center purchase agreements have a history of a cycle in any given year the forward contractual obligations on the expense side (not performance obligations owed to Microsoft) are dominated by construction obligations and purchase agreements related to data centers as described in the footnotes as 'commitments primarily relate to datacenters and include open purchase order and take-or-pay contracts that are not presented as construction commitments,'” analyst Jackson Ader wrote in a note to clients. 

Ader, who has an Overweight rating and $575 price target on Microsoft, added that purchase agreements for fiscal 2025 make up 61.8% of the non-debt-related commitments, compared to 27.1% for fiscal 2025.

“The fact that so much of the following year's spend comes from construction and purchase agreements does signal that there is flexibility in the company's plans since the next year's commitments generally make up 90%+ of the total commitments from that line item—these aren't long-term commitments.” 

As such, Ader said he is “encouraged” by Microsoft's “vocal willingness” to be flexible on its capex spending, and the recent reports should be seen as a positive for shareholders.

data.title: China escalates trade war with 84% tariffs on US goods - reports
data.description:
In response to the U.S.'s 104% tariffs on Chinese goods, Beijing will raise its tariff on U.S. goods to 84%, effective April 10, the government said on Wednesday, according to media reports.

That increased from China's existing 34% levy on U.S. goods entering the country, the Associated Press said. President Donald Trump responded to the 34% tariff by imposing an additional 50% tariff on Chinese goods, bringing the total tariff to 104%.

The move aligns with China's previous statement that it will "fight to the end"  if the U.S. persists with its tariff policy. 

China also added six firms to its unreliable entity list and 12 U.S. entities to its export control list, Bloomberg reported, citing the Chinese government's statement.

The Chinese yuan (CNY:USD) weakened 0.1% against the U.S. dollar. 

Developing... check back for updates.

Related tickers: (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (FLCH), (CNYA), (YINN), (ASHR)

data.title: Shopify, Affirm drive global expansion of Shop Pay
data.description:
• Shopify (NASDAQ:SHOP) merchants in Canada with early access can now offer Shop Pay Installments, exclusively powered by Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM), to shoppers, marking the product’s first availability outside the U.S.
  • Shop Pay Installments is a buy now, pay later option that lets customers pay for their purchases in installments instead of paying the full amount upfront.
  • The payment method will become available in general access to Shopify merchants in Canada and the U.K. this summer, with cross-border commerce capabilities between the U.S., Canada, and U.K. to follow.
  • Additionally, the companies plan to expand to Australia and Western Europe next, starting with France, Germany and the Netherlands.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: China's white paper on U.S. trade: Ports, aircraft, rice and beef
data.description:
While China has indicated that it is willing to resolve differences with Washington through "equal-footed dialogue," it called out the U.S. for causing "multiple obstacles" to trade between the two countries.

In a white paper on its U.S.-China ties, Beijing detailed how President Donald Trump's tariffs would upend world trade, ramp up inflationary pressures in the U.S. and weaken the American industrial base instead of reshoring manufacturing.

China insisted that it has adhered to the 2020 trade agreement between the two nations, but the U.S. has hindered trade. "Limited U.S. production capacity hindered exports to China," it said, pointing to lower aircraft deliveries from Boeing (BA) and poor quality of U.S. wheat exported to China in 2020.

"Inadequate infrastructure has contributed to elevated transport costs," China claimed, noting how most U.S. ports in the Gulf of Mexico cannot accommodate very large crude carriers.

Beijing also called out the "limited competitiveness" of U.S. products. "U.S. soybeans are at a price disadvantage compared to South American soybeans; U.S. beef is significantly more expensive (roughly 50% higher than South American beef); U.S. rice can hardly compete with Southeast Asian rice in terms of quality, appearance, taste, and price."

"China does not deliberately pursue a trade surplus," it affirmed, saying it's rather "an inevitable result of the structural issues in the U.S. economy and a consequence of the comparative advantages and international division of labor between the two countries."

data.title: Walmart leans into uncertainty by backing Q1 guidance despite the economic backdrop
data.description:
Walmart (NYSE:WMT) released a guidance update as part of its Investment Community Meeting being held on Wednesday. 

For Q1, the retail giant continues to expect sales growth of +3% to +4%. Walmart (NYSE:WMT) also held its annual sales and operating income growth guidance at their prior levels. The company noted that the range of outcomes for Q1 operating income growth has widened due to less favorable category mix, higher casualty claims expense, and the desire to maintain flexibility to invest in price as tariffs are implemented.

"History tells us that when we lean into these periods of uncertainty, Walmart emerges on the other side with greater share and a stronger business," stated Walmart Chief Financial Officer John David Rainey.

The event is expected to see Walmart (WMT) leadership share the company's focus on driving growth and creating shareholder value. The meeting will also highlight Walmart's (WMT) people-led, tech-powered omnichannel strategy, and why it is uniquely positioned to sustain attractive growth and create shareholder returns.

In terms of strategy, Walmart (WMT) plans to drive growth by improving customer and member experiences. The Bentonville-based company wants to create shareholder value by strengthening its business model.

Shares of Walmart (WMT) were down 1.6% in premarket trading amid broad selling pressure in the market. Target (TGT) was down 2.0% in the early session, while Costco Wholesale (COST) slipped 0.4%.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Delta Air Lines Q2 EPS lags estimates, cuts planned capacity growth in H2
European markets plummet as Trump tariffs take effect
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.00am ET.

London (UKX) -2.92% to 7,681. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -2.90% to 19,691.

France (CAC:IND) -2.89% to 6,895.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 3.29% lower to 470.91, reversing gains from the previous as trade tensions intensified after Trump's sweeping tariffs, including a cumulative 104% levy on Chinese imports. Losses were broad-based, with all sectors in negative territory. Pharmaceuticals, oil & gas, basic resources, and real estate led the declines. Meanwhile, Spain's Prime Minister, Pedro Sánchez, called on the European Union to review its relationship with China.

European natural gas futures hit its lowest level since September as tariffs fueled fears of a global economic slowdown and weaker energy demand.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic calendar today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 11 basis points at 4.37%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point at 2.63%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 8 basis points to 4.68%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: Peabody reviewing options for $3.78B Anglo American coal deal after mine fire
data.description:
Peabody Energy (NYSE:BTU) said Wednesday it is reviewing all options related to its $3.78B agreement to acquire Anglo American's (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) steelmaking coal operations following a fire at an Australian mine.

According to media reports, production at Anglo's (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) Moranbah North mine in Queensland was suspended after an underground fire broke out at the mine last week.

Peabody (NYSE:BTU) said it remains in conversation with Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) to better understand the impacts of the event and would preserve all rights and protections under its purchase agreements.

Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said it continues to work with Peabody (BTU) on completing the transaction and that conditions in the mine remain stable as it works to facilitate re-entry into it.

Peabody's (BTU) deal for the Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) assets included an upfront payment of $2.05B at completion, deferred cash consideration of $725M and another potential $550M, as well as a $450M contingent cash consideration linked to the reopening of the Grosvenor mine, where production was suspended in June, also following a fire; the dea; was expected to be finalized by Q3.

Peabody (BTU) +17.1% pre-market Wednesday, adding to a 9.2% gain on Tuesday, as President Trump signed executive orders intended to expand the production and use of coal in the U.S.

data.title: Bristol-Myers gets Pomalyst antitrust case dismissed
data.description:
A federal judge in New York has dismissed a proposed class action filed by health insurer Blue Cross Blue Shield of Louisiana alleging that Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) ran an illegal scheme to protect its monopoly over its blockbuster multiple myeloma treatment Pomalyst.

U.S. District Judge Edgardo Ramos in Manhattan dismissed the case, noting that the plaintiffs had failed to establish that the company had violated the federal Sherman antitrust law, Reuters reported, citing a 70-page court document made public on Tuesday.

According to the judge, the plaintiff purchasers had not shown that Bristol Myers (NYSE:BMY) had fraudulently obtained six patents linked to Pomalyst. Additionally, they hadn't established how the company’s nine lawsuits filed against generic drugmakers Teva (TEVA) and Viatris’ (VTRS) predecessor Mylan in 2017-2020 were "objectively baseless" and were the reason for fraudulent settlements.

Added to Bristol Myers’ (NYSE:BMY) portfolio through its 2019 acquisition of Celgene, Pomalyst, also known as pomalidomide, generated $3.55B in revenue for the company in 2024, making up roughly 7% of its topline.

data.title: Neogen misses Q3 top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Neogen press release (NASDAQ:NEOG): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $221M (-3.4% Y/Y) misses by $3.23M.
  • Core revenue, which excludes the impacts of foreign currency translation, as well as acquisitions completed and product lines discontinued in the last 12 months, increased by 0.2%. 
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $48.5 million.

  • Fiscal Year 2025 Outlook

    The Company is updating its full-year outlook, primarily due to third-quarter results being lower than expected and the effect of the rising level of macroeconomic uncertainty on the Company’s end markets, as well as the expected impact of tariffs in the fourth quarter. Revenue is now expected to be approximately $895 million (vs. $910.17M consensus) and Adjusted EBITDA is now expected to be approximately $195 million. The Company now expects capital expenditures to be approximately $100 million, reflecting the lower Adjusted EBITDA and pull-forward of some level of integration capex into fiscal 2025.
